perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives;
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information shall mean the following: a) all such information, of any kind whatsoever (whether in oral, written or electronic form, and including, but not limited to, technical, commercial, financial, accounting, legal and administrative information) pertaining to the Sale of the Munt and the Sell ers as may be provided to the Disclosee and their responsible managers, officers, employees , shareholders, members of the Board of Directors and advisors (including financial, legal and tax advisors and auditors) (“ Representatives ”), by the Seller s, their advisors or their representatives, except for information already known by the Disclosee prior to the execution of this agreement;
perturbation.explanation: By adding the exception 'except for information already known by the Disclosee prior to the execution of this agreement,' the contract contradicts the broad definition of 'Confidential Information,' potentially leading to disputes about what information falls under the confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Belgian Code of Economic Law, Book XI, Title 3, Chapter 1
perturbation.law_citation: Article XI.331 of the Belgian Code of Economic Law
perturbation.law_explanation: The Belgian Code of Economic Law broadly protects confidential business information, including know-how and trade secrets. The modified text creates an ambiguous exception that weakens this protection, potentially allowing the Disclosee to disclose information merely because they already knew it, even if it's still confidential trade information belonging to the Sellers. This directly contradicts the intention of safeguarding trade secrets, as prior knowledge should not automatically negate confidentiality obligations when it pertains to trade secrets acquired outside of a legal disclosure.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Justel databank Andere informatie en diensten van de overheid: www.belgium.be
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to the definition of 'Confidential Information' for information already known by the Disclosee. The Law Explanation states that this weakens the protection of confidential business information and trade secrets under Belgian law because prior knowledge should not automatically negate confidentiality obligations. The absence of supporting law snippets does not negate the assessment. Because the altered term creates an exception to the original obligations, contradicting the intentions of protecting trade secrets, and fits under the Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction definition, this creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall immediately notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the Disclosee or any of its Representatives becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Confidential Information to a regulatory authority or to any other entity or third party, the Disclosee shall use its best efforts to notify the Sellers before disclosing such Confidential In formation, so that the Sellers may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, without prejudice to t he Disclosee’s remedies under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'shall immediately notify' with 'shall use its best efforts to notify' weakens the obligation to notify, introducing uncertainty. In certain instances, immediate notification is required to preserve legal protections for confidential information. Replacing “immediately notify” introduces wiggle room for the Disclosee to potentially delay notification.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Belgian Law of 30 July 2018 on the protection of trade secrets
perturbation.law_citation: Article 4 of the Belgian Law of 30 July 2018
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 4 of the Belgian Law of 30 July 2018 requires measures to preserve the confidentiality of trade secrets, including timely notification in cases of compelled disclosure. Replacing 'shall immediately notify' with 'shall use its best efforts to notify' weakens this obligation, creating the possibility that the Disclosee's actions might not be sufficient to preserve the trade secret, thus contradicting the law's intent.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Justel databank Andere informatie en diensten van de overheid: www.belgium.be Naar boven Naar boven Titel 2018013207 30 JUILLET 2018. - Arrêté royal modifiant l'arrêté royal du 25 avril 2002 relatif à la fixation et à la liquidation du budget des moyens financiers des hôpitaux Bron: Sante Publique, Sécurité de la Chaine Alimentaire et Environnement Publicatie: 9 augustus 2018 Nummer: 2018013207 bladzijde: 62549 Dossiernummer: 2018-07-30/08 Inwerkingtreding : 1 september 2018 Deze tekst wijzigt de volgende tekst: 2002022335 Inhoudstafel Art. 1-5 Tekst Article 1er . Dans l'article 15 de l'arrêté royal du 25 avril 2002 relatif à la fixation et à la liquidation du budget des moyens financiers des hôpitaux, 37°, modifié en dernier lieu par l'arrêté royal du 26 novembre 2010, est remplacé par ce qui suit : " 37° le financement d'une prime annuelle octroyée à certains infirmiers selon les conditions définies dans l'arrêté royal du 28 décembre 2011 relatif à l'exécution du plan d'attractivité pour la profession infirmière, dans certains secteurs fédéraux de la santé, en ce qui concerne les primes pour des titres et qualifications professionnels particuliers et les prestations inconfortables ; ". Art. 2 . Dans l'article 71, du même arrêté, les modifications suivantes sont apportées : 1° dans le paragraphe 1er, modifié par l'arrêté royal du 6 septembre 2016, les mots " l'arrêté royal du 22 juin 2010 relatif à l'exécution du plan attractivité de la profession infirmière concernant les primes des titres et qualifications professionnels particuliers et des prestations inconfortables " sont remplacés par les mots " l'arrêté royal du 28 décembre 2011 relatif à l'exécution du plan d'attractivité pour la profession infirmière, dans certains secteurs fédéraux de la santé, en ce qui concerne les primes pour des titres et qualifications professionnels particuliers et les prestations inconfortables " ; 2° l'article est complété par ce qui suit : " § 3. A partir du 1er septembre 2018, le financement prévu au paragraphe 1er n'est plus octroyé à l'hôpital relevant du champ d'application de la commission paritaire des é
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "shall immediately notify," creating a clear and mandatory obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to "shall use its best efforts to notify." The Law Explanation states that Belgian Law requires measures to preserve the confidentiality of trade secrets, including timely notification in cases of compelled disclosure. The change introduces uncertainty about whether the notification will be timely, potentially violating the intent of the law. While neither snippet directly quotes the law itself, the Law Explanation states that the original text aligns with the legal expectation of immediate notification, and that the changed text reduces the likelihood of compliance.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with Belgian law, except for matters concerning intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware, USA, and the Parties irrevocably agree to submit themselves to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Courts of Brussels.
perturbation.explanation: Introducing an exception for intellectual property matters to be governed by Delaware law creates a conflict. The core of the agreement revolves around Confidential Information, much of which could constitute trade secrets, a form of intellectual property. This change risks forum shopping and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Belgian Code of Private International Law (Wetboek IPR)
perturbation.law_citation: Article 105 of the Belgian Code of Private International Law
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 105 of the Belgian Code of Private International Law allows parties to choose the applicable law for contractual obligations, however, it is usually interpreted within the bounds of reasonableness and connection to the contract's primary subject matter. Introducing Delaware law for IP matters, when the main contract is about Belgian real estate and general confidentiality in Belgium, could be seen as an attempt to circumvent Belgian law. This raises potential enforceability issues in Belgian courts, as Article 105 would be balanced against the potential evasion of mandatory Belgian laws concerning IP protection and trade secrets in the context of a contract with a clear Belgian nexus.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Trans-Lex.org This page uses so called "cookies" to improve its service (i.e. "tracking"). perturbation.justification: The original contract stipulates that Belgian law governs the entire agreement. The changed text introduces an exception, stating that Delaware law will govern intellectual property matters. The Perturbation Explanation highlights that this change could lead to forum shopping and uncertainty, particularly since the contract revolves around confidential information, some of which may be trade secrets. The Law Explanation suggests that introducing Delaware law for IP matters, when the main contract relates to Belgian real estate and general confidentiality in Belgium, could be seen as an attempt to circumvent Belgian law and raise potential enforceability issues. Given that 'Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction' arises when contractual language deviates from legally recognized definitions, making the contract unenforceable, and that the modified text introduces a different jurisdiction's law for specific aspects, it creates a legal contradiction because it deviates from the original, unified legal framework and creates potential for legal challenges regarding which jurisdiction's law applies to which aspects of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have, any brea ch of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury. In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.changed_text: It is further understood and agreed that, without prejudice to any other rights or remedies which either party may have, any brea ch of these undertakings could cause the Sellers and/or its shareholders irreparable injury. In the event of an actual or threatened breach by the Disclosee and/or its Representatives of any provisions of this Agreement , the Sellers shall be entitled (i) t o seek injunctive relief in any court of competent jurisdiction restraining the Disclosee and/or its Representatives from breaching the terms hereof or from disclosing any Confidential Information to any person, provided that the Disclosee acted with malicious intent and (ii) specific performance and other equit able relief.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the requirement of 'malicious intent' to trigger injunctive relief imposes a higher burden of proof on the Sellers. Under Belgian law, injunctive relief for breach of contract, especially regarding confidentiality, does not necessarily require malicious intent. It typically focuses on the fact of the breach and the potential for irreparable harm.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Article 584 of the Belgian Judicial Code
perturbation.law_citation: Article 584 of the Belgian Judicial Code
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 584 of the Belgian Judicial Code allows a judge to order provisional measures, including injunctive relief, in urgent cases to prevent imminent harm. Adding a 'malicious intent' requirement contradicts this by making it more difficult for the Sellers to obtain urgent injunctive relief, even if a breach of confidentiality is causing significant harm, thereby undermining the effectiveness of the legal remedy.
perturbation.location: Section regarding remedies for breach of agreement

Find sources: "Belgian Judicial Code" – news · newspapers · books · scholar · JSTOR ( October 2020 ) ( Learn how and when to remove this message ) Belgian Judicial Code Belgian Federal Parliament Territorial extent Belgium Passed by Belgian Chamber of Rep. Passed 22 June 1967 Passed by Belgian Senate Passed 29 June 1967 Royal assent 10 October 1967 Commenced 1 November 1970 Summary This code governs the organisation of the courts and tribunals of Belgium, their jurisdiction and their procedure. Status: In force The Belgian Judicial Code ( Dutch : Gerechtelijk Wetboek , French : Code Judiciaire , German : Gerichtsgesetzbuch ) is a code of law in the country of Belgium , formally adopted on 10 October 1967 and currently still in force. The Judicial Code governs the organisation of the courts and tribunals of the Belgian judiciary , their jurisdiction , as well as the applicable rules of civil procedure . As such, the Judicial Code is one of the important codes of law in the Belgian legal system . [ 1 ] In criminal proceedings however, the jurisdiction and rules of procedure of the courts and tribunals of Belgium are governed by the Belgian Code of Criminal Procedure . [ 1 ] History [ edit ] The proposed law containing the Judicial Code was passed by the Belgian Chamber of Representatives on 22 June 1967 and by the Belgian Senate on 29 June 1967. [ 2 ] The adopted law was subsequently promulgated by the King of the Belgians on 10 October 1967, and entered into force on 1 November 1970. The Judicial Code has been amended many times since. Contents [ edit ] Part I: General principles [ edit ] → Link to the Dutch text and the French text in the database of consolidated Belgian legislation (Justel). This part of the code (articles 1–57) contains general provisions regarding the adjudication of cases and the value of judgments and rulings . Part II: Judicial organization [ edit ] → Link to the Dutch text and the French text in the database of consolidated Belgian legislation (Justel). This part of the code (articles 58–555/16) consists of six 'books': Book I: Bodies of the judiciary (articles 58–287novies) establishes the bodies that constitute the judiciary, and governs the appointment and career of judicial officers and court personnel; Book II: Judicial offices (articles 288–427quater) governs the duties, the remuneration and the retirement of judicial officers and court personnel, as well as disciplinary procedures regarding their offices and professions; Book III: Bar (articles 428–508) governs the practice of the profession of attorney , and establishes their professional bodies; Book IIIbis: First-line and second-line legal aid (articles 508/1–508/25) regulates the provision of first-line and second-line legal aid ; Book IV: Court bailiffs (articles 509–555quinquies) governs the practice of the office of court bailiff , and establishes their professional bodies; Book V: Court experts and sworn translators, interpreters and translator-interpreters (articles 555/6–555/16) governs the practice of the professions of court expert , translator , interpreter and translator-interpreter. Part III: Competence [ edit ] → Link to the Dutch text and the French text in the database of consolidated Belgian legislation (Justel). This part of the code (articles 556–663) lays down the jurisdiction of each of the courts and tribunal
perturbation.justification: The original contract allows the Sellers to seek injunctive relief for breaches of the agreement. The changed contract adds 'provided that the Disclosee acted with malicious intent,' thus requiring proof of malicious intent to obtain injunctive relief. According to the Law Explanation, Article 584 of the Belgian Judicial Code allows a judge to order provisional measures, including injunctive relief, in urgent cases to prevent imminent harm. The change contradicts this because it makes it more difficult for the Sellers to obtain urgent injunctive relief, even if a breach of confidentiality is causing significant harm. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is missing, Scraped Law Snippet 2 references the Belgian Judicial Code. The added requirement directly undermines the possibility of obtaining injunctive relief urgently, as proof of intent would need to be established, thus creating a direct legal contradiction.
